metrics.narrative: Russell has named target outcomes -- an autonomous negotiation agent expected to deliver 3-5% savings on tactical tail spend, and cutting manual invoice verification from ~10-15% today toward near-zero exceptions (13 Mar 2026 demo, key_takeaways/impact notes). These are real, buyer-anchored figures, not generic vendor claims. However, the NDA-bound ROI business case Zycus committed to build for Russell (26 Mar 2026 action item) has no confirmed delivery anywhere in the record through 7 Jul 2026, so a validated, Russell-specific business case is still outstanding.

sources: Task email, 2026-07-07 (Gajendra Badodekar to Rick Taranek)
champion.narrative: Gajendra Badodekar (Director, Global Sourcing & Procurement) is a clear, active champion: he drove the RFP, pushed back hard when Zycus proposed removing CLM ('I guess that I was misunderstood during the demo... please share the updated quote today itself,' 30 Jun 2026), chased pricing urgently ahead of stakeholder meetings, and is personally coordinating the internal stakeholder debrief as of 7 Jul 2026. His limitation is not engagement but authority: he has stated CLM/scope tradeoffs will be settled 'during negotiations' with shortlisted vendors, implying a higher approval layer he does not control alone.

competition.narrative: Agiloft is the named, live competitive threat, specifically for the CLM scope: Zycus's own 24 Jun 2026 proposal initially dropped CLM pricing on the premise that 'Agiloft could provide comparable effectiveness,' but the champion reversed this on 30 Jun 2026, saying CLM must be priced back in because the vendor decision on CLM will be made 'during negotiations' with finalists -- i.e., Agiloft is still actively in the running for that scope. Concur is named as an incumbent AP/T&E system Zycus would integrate with rather than displace outright. The original RFP field of 5 vendors was narrowed to 3 per the 15 Jun 2026 note, and further in-person negotiation in Seattle is planned for the finalists; the identities of the other 1-2 finalists beyond Agiloft are not named anywhere in the record.

sources: Avoma call notes, 2026-03-13 (Demo with Zycus for P2P/S2P)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively and specifically articulated across two deep-read demos: procurement and AP processes are 'force-fit' into Concur and Agiloft with manual handoffs (13 Mar 2026, current_workflow/pain_point notes); sourcing/RFX runs offline in Excel with no template library; contract redlining is done on offline Word documents with no clause library or automated risk checks; AP still manually verifies 10-15% of invoices. This is buyer-articulated, not rep-inferred, and confirmed by multiple named speakers.

sources: Task email, 2026-07-01 (Rick Taranek to Gajendra Badodekar, proposal wCLM sent)
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or contracting activity has started -- the deal is still at commercial-proposal stage, with pricing being revised as recently as 1 Jul 2026 (adding iContract/CLM licensing and implementation cost back in). This is expected at this point in the motion but means the entire contracting relay (legal, InfoSec, supplier onboarding, signature) has zero runway against the recorded 17 Jul 2026 close date.

sources: Task email, 2026-06-30 (Gajendra Badodekar to Rick Taranek)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or C-level/VP sponsor has appeared on any of the five discovered calls; Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false on the record, and the field-recorded Why_not_Zycus__c notes ('Decision-making authority not yet identified') is stale but directionally still true. Champion Gajendra Badodekar is Director-level and has stated in email (30 Jun 2026) that CLM scope decisions will be worked out 'with short-listed vendors during negotiations' -- implying he does not have unilateral authority to finalize scope or budget. With a vendor-selection decision reportedly imminent (within ~1 week per the 7 Jul 2026 Next Step), the absence of a mapped budget approver is the single largest structural gap.

decision_process.narrative: The process cadence is unusually well documented in Next_Step_History__c: discovery (Feb-Mar 2026) -> NDA + RFI questionnaire (Mar-Apr) -> formal RFP submitted 7 May 2026 -> demo rounds (10/16/17 Jun 2026, one cancelled) -> vendor shortlist (originally 5, narrowed to 3 per the 15 Jun 2026 note) -> commercial proposal iteration (23 Jun-1 Jul 2026) -> internal stakeholder debrief now underway, with the champion targeting a decision around 15 Jul 2026, and a planned in-person final negotiation round in Seattle for down-selected vendors. The mechanics of who signs off internally are not yet named.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well understood from two full demo transcripts and a formal RFP: AI capability is rated 'Very Critical' per AIS_Why__c, with specific asks around clause-level AI search/export, configurable global-vs-local clause standards, conceptual (not literal) redlining risk detection, and open-source AI-agent integration. Sourcing, CLM, AP/expense, spend analytics and supplier onboarding were all scoped in detail across the 13 Mar and 26 Mar 2026 demos. What remains unconfirmed is the buyer's internal scoring/weighting mechanism between finalists -- Zycus has not been shown the RFP scorecard itself.

value.how_we_win: Close the still-open live AI-redlining demo and clause-library configurability proof points the legal lead asked for on 2026-06-16, and keep CLM priced and positioned as part of a single unified platform rather than a stand-alone module Agiloft can match feature-for-feature.

value.name: Agiloft
value.quote: We currently use Agiloft for contract management; we are evaluating whether to expand one of them or bring in a new unified platform like Zycus. (Gajendra, 2026-03-26 discovery)
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2026-03-26, SF Competitors__c field
value.how_we_win: Deliver live AI redlining demo Karishma requested (prove contract-AI works, not roadmap). Anchor unified-platform ROI (one vendor, one data model, no integration tax vs Agiloft+Concur+legacy AP stack). Escalate Merlin agentic negotiation and intake as differentiation Agiloft cannot match, make it Phase 2 centerpiece so even if CLM deprioritized in Phase 1, Merlin story pulls it back.

value.name: Agiloft
value.quote: I guess that I was misunderstood during the demo when I said that we could keep the CLM optional - this was something that we will discuss with short-listed vendors during negotiations.
value.how_we_win: Reframe CLM as an AI-redlining upgrade over Agiloft's manual, offline review process (live InstaReview risk checks, conceptual deviation detection Karishma preferred on 2026-03-26) rather than positioning coexistence; keep iContract fully priced through the shortlist decision.

value.name: Agiloft
value.quote: Karishma (legal) on June 16 demo call explicitly requested 'Show live working version of AI redlining assistance (not roadmap)' to validate Zycus contract AI capabilities
value.how_we_win: Deliver live working version of Merlin AI redlining to Karishma before July 15 selection deadline, demonstrating clause identification, contract review automation, and open-source AI integration support. Position Zycus as the unified P2P + CLM + AI platform that reduces integration complexity versus point solutions. Make Merlin the centerpiece of the shortlist recommendation.

value.name: Agiloft

value.source: Avoma demo call 2026-03-13, Karishma Muravne (legal lead)
value.how_we_win: Prove AI-driven contract intelligence (Instareview, clause library configurability, conceptual deviation detection) and end-to-end S2P integration that Agiloft + Concur + Excel cannot deliver; anchor ROI on touchless invoicing, 3-5% tactical savings, and PO-to-pay cycle compression (Broadridge reference: 2 weeks → 3.9 days).
